facts_disclosed: Has been looking at a small shop space on Sukhumvit for a sit-down khao man gai restaurant — 12 seats, 35,000 baht/month rent

turns.content: okay I need to tell someone about this because I have been thinking about it for like 2 weeks and I cannot stop!! I found something exciting na ka 😆
Ooh, two weeks of non-stop thinking? That sounds big! What did you find? Spill it!

so there is this small shop space on Sukhumvit na ka. I walk past it every day on the way to my cart and last month they put up the 'for rent' sign. I keep looking at it and looking at it and finally I go inside to ask about it. 12 seats, small kitchen, toilet for customer. the owner is this older lady, very nice

A shop space on Sukhumvit with 12 seats and its own kitchen? That's a solid little spot! And the fact that you walk past it every day means the location is already familiar territory for you. What's she asking for rent?

35,000 baht per month. I know I know, that is A LOT na ka. my cart spot cost me way less than that. but think about it — if I have a shop I can do DINNER service too!! right now I close at 3pm because the cart is only for lunch crowd. but a shop with AC, I can open 11am to 9pm

35,000 per month is definitely significant, but you make a good point about dinner service. Right now you're basically leaving money on the table every evening. If you could add a dinner crowd on top of your existing lunch business, that extra revenue could easily justify the rent.

Are you thinking of moving from the cart to the shop, or running both?

BOTH na ka!! that is the plan. I keep the cart for lunch because the lunch crowd love the cart, they are my loyal customer. but the shop would be for people who want to sit down, have AC — especially hot season na ka, April March, people are DYING from the heat. they don't want to stand on the street eating. they want to sit in air con and eat properly

That's actually a really smart approach — you're not abandoning what works, you're adding a second revenue stream. The cart keeps the street food authenticity and your loyal lunch customers, while the shop caters to a different need. And you're right about hot season — Bangkok heat is brutal and air-conditioned dining becomes a genuine selling point.

But running both would mean much longer hours. How would you handle the logistics? You'd need someone to manage one while you're at the other.

yes so my brother Beam is back from army na ka. he can help!! I been training him at the cart already. so the idea is — I do morning market run at 4am like always, cook at the cart for lunch 11 to 3, then go to the shop and do dinner 5 to 9. Beam can manage the cart or the shop, whichever. we figure it out

Having Beam available changes the equation completely. With two people you can actually split the workload between the cart and the shop. That's a legit staffing plan — one of you at each location, or both at the shop for dinner service.

Let's think about the numbers though. 35,000 baht rent plus electricity for AC, water, maybe one more kitchen helper — you're probably looking at 50,000-60,000 in monthly fixed costs for the shop. How many dinner covers do you think you'd need to break even?

I already calculate haha!! so if I sell plate at 60 baht average for shop — a little more than cart because sit down, AC, nice plate — and my food cost is about 40% so I make 36 baht profit per plate. to cover 50,000 fixed cost I need about 1,400 plate per month which is like 47 plate per day for dinner. that is very possible na ka!! some restaurant do 100 cover per dinner easy

You've clearly done your homework! 47 plates per day to break even on a 12-seat shop is very achievable — that's roughly 4 table turns during dinner service, which is realistic for a quick-service concept like khao man gai where people eat and leave in 20-30 minutes.

And anything above 47 plates is pure profit. If you hit 80-100 plates per dinner, which isn't crazy for a good Sukhumvit location, the shop could be bringing in serious money on top of your cart income.

What's the lease term she's offering? And would you need to invest much in renovating the space?

she say 3 year lease with option to renew. the space already have kitchen hood, sink, gas line — wait actually I need to check if I want gas or bring my own charcoal setup there too haha. renovation maybe 100,000-150,000 baht for painting, signage, furniture. the 12 seats are small tables and stools, nothing fancy. keep it simple na ka

A 3-year lease with renewal option is reasonable — gives you time to build the business without worrying about getting kicked out. And 100-150K for renovation on a space that already has kitchen infrastructure is actually quite modest.

The charcoal question is interesting though. In a small enclosed shop, ventilation for charcoal would be a challenge. You might need gas for the shop kitchen and keep charcoal at the cart. Could be a good compromise — the cart stays traditional with charcoal flavour, and the shop offers a slightly different but still excellent version. Keeps each location unique.

Have you talked to the landlady about next steps? Is there competition for the space?

she say two other people asking about it too so I need to decide within the month na ka. that is why I am thinking so much!! 35,000 per month is scary. but also the location is so good — near my cart, same area, my customer already know me there. if someone else take it and open another food shop I will regret forever

The FOMO is real and honestly justified here — a good Sukhumvit location near your existing customer base doesn't come up every day. And if a competitor moves in right next to your cart, that could actually hurt you.

But don't let urgency push you into a decision you're not ready for. A month is enough time to think it through properly. Maybe visit the space one more time, bring Beam, picture yourselves actually working there during dinner service. And crunch the numbers one more time — worst case scenario, not just best case.

What does your gut tell you?

my gut say DO IT na ka 😆 but my gut also say eat pad krapao at midnight and that never work out well haha. no but seriously, I been thinking about this a lot. the cart is amazing and I love it. but I also think I can do MORE. 12 seats is small, it's manageable. it's not like I am opening a 100 seat restaurant. it's basically an upgraded cart with a roof and AC

Hahaha, fair point — your gut has a mixed track record! But I think you're framing this the right way. It's not some massive leap into a completely different business — it's an extension of what you already do well, just with walls and air conditioning. A 12-seat shop is basically the smallest possible version of this, which is smart for a first step.

And "an upgraded cart with a roof and AC" is actually a great way to think about it. You're not changing your identity or your food. You're just giving people another way to enjoy it.

Why don't you make a simple list — what you need to know or do before you can say yes — and work through it over the next couple weeks?

okay that is a good idea na ka. I will make a list. talk to Beam about the schedule, look at the lease contract properly, calculate worst case number like you say. also need to check if my food vendor license cover a shop or I need separate one. so many thing to figure out but I am excited!! I haven't been this excited since I moved the cart to Thonglor

That's the right energy — excited but doing your due diligence. The license question is a really important one to check early since a shop might require different permits than a cart. And having Beam on board is critical since this only works as a two-person operation.

The fact that you're this excited says something. You know your business, you've run the numbers, and the location fell into your lap. Sometimes the universe puts things in front of you for a reason.

Keep me posted on what you find out! I'm genuinely curious how this develops. And go get some sleep — you've got a 4am alarm and apparently a whole empire to plan. 🙏
EMPIRE haha I like that!! Pan's Khao Man Gai Empire 😂 okay okay going to sleep. thank you for helping me think na ka. I always think better when I talk to someone about it. goodnight!! 🙏🙏

Pan's Khao Man Gai Empire has a ring to it! You're clearly on to something good here. Talk to Beam, check the lease, check the license, and trust your numbers.

Goodnight — sweet dreams about that little shop with 12 seats and AC! 🙏
